title: Slice 2 — --explain CLI: print why each task in the plan would run or skip
description: Surface slice 1's decision records to the user. 'racket src/main.rkt --explain <target>' walks the plan (like --commands, sharing plan-suffix/--from scoping) and prints one line per task: the decision and its reason, naming changed inputs by artifact. This upgrades the current one-glyph dry-run legend (≡/≈/▶) into an actual explanation. Also decide here how 'conditional' propagation is explained: a task downstream of one that would run is stale *because* its upstream is — the explanation should say which upstream, giving a one-hop provenance chain through the plan.
acceptance_criteria: --explain <target> prints a per-task reason including the specific changed input or the upstream task that makes it conditionally stale; output scoped by --from like --commands.
close_reason: --explain prints per-task reason with named inputs/outputs; conditional skips name the upstream that causes them; pure walk-explanations unit-tested; 99 tests pass

title: Slice 1 — reasons, not booleans: cache layer returns a decision record
description: Today cache.rkt conflates its answers: input-fingerprint returns #f for three different reasons (boundary task, unresolvable input, missing file) and cache-hit? returns a bare boolean that folds together no-entry, version mismatch, fingerprint mismatch, and missing outputs. Provenance needs the distinctions. Replace the boolean core with a pure decision record — e.g. (run 'boundary), (run 'input-changed (field.csv)), (run 'recipe-changed), (run 'no-cache-entry), (run 'output-missing occurrences.db), (skip 'cached) — keeping cache-hit? as a thin wrapper so existing callers don't churn. To name WHICH input changed, the cache entry must store per-input hashes, not just the combined fingerprint (cache format is versioned; bumping CACHE-VERSION is a designed-for cache miss, not a migration). Unit tests over synthetic graphs in the model-test.rkt style.
acceptance_criteria: A pure function returns a structured why-run/why-skip decision for any task; changed inputs are named individually; existing --build/--commands behavior unchanged; raco test passes.
close_reason: cache layer returns structured decision records; per-input hashes in v2 entries name the changed input; cache-hit? kept as thin wrapper; 89 tests pass

title: Provenance, first-class: the build explains why it rebuilds (H1)
description: First Horizon 1 slice (ROADMAP.md). Horizon 0 built the machinery that decides what runs — reachability, input fingerprints, cache hits, boundary exemptions — but every decision is made and thrown away. This epic surfaces those decisions as a queryable property: 'why did this task rebuild?' / 'why is this artifact stale?'. It is the third application of the Datalog layer (after reachability and its cross-check) and pays down debugging cost for early cutoff and broader target coverage, which come next.
acceptance_criteria: For any task in a target's plan, stelis can answer why it would run or why it was skipped — naming the specific changed input, changed recipe, missing cache entry, missing output, or boundary status — both as a dry-run query and after a real --build.
close_reason: all four slices landed: decision records, --explain, Datalog why-stale chains, persisted build trace

title: Gate: verify occurrences.db reproducible (fix is beeatlas-zo7)
description: Reference/gate, not a fix. The nondeterminism is a beeatlas problem, tracked and fixed there as beeatlas-zo7 (dbt occurrences mart lacks stable ordering). This Stelis issue exists only to gate the st-d44 epic exit condition ('minimal-upstream rebuild proven reproducible'): it stays OPEN until 'racket src/main.rkt --verify --from dbt-build occurrences.db' reports DETERMINISTIC. Full diagnosis is on beeatlas-zo7. Discovered by the st-d44.6 harness.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS (localized). Ran dbt twice + generate-sqlite twice from a fixed parquet:
- occurrences.parquet: NONDETERMINISTIC across dbt runs (2c2f64a6 vs 40194a14) — ROOT CAUSE.
- occurrence_places.parquet: DETERMINISTIC (same hash) — so not all dbt output; specific to the occurrences model.
- generate-sqlite from a FIXED parquet: DETERMINISTIC (47b2b5c5 == 47b2b5c5) — the SQLite export is clean; it faithfully propagates its input.
Conclusion: nondeterminism lives entirely in beeatlas's dbt 'occurrences' mart (occurrences.sql or an int_ dependency) — classic DuckDB-parallelism row-order. Likely fix (beeatlas-side, separate session): stable ORDER BY on the final occurrences select / the COPY-to-parquet, or pin duckdb threads. occurrence_places already being stable is a hint (compare what it does differently).
VERIFIED FIXED. racket src/main.rkt --verify --from dbt-build occurrences.db now reports DETERMINISTIC — both builds byte-identical (sha1 761898069fa11d21..., 33574912 B). beeatlas-zo7's fix confirmed by the harness. This unblocks the st-d44 exit condition (minimal-upstream rebuild proven reproducible).

title: Slice 2b — execute one cheap task via subprocess
description: Run generate-sqlite through its recipe as a real subprocess (uv/3.14), capturing exit status. Network/secret-free proof of the execution mechanism. Streaming logs (st-d44.5), partial success (st-d44.4), secrets, and the full ingestion chain are out of scope.
notes: DONE. src/exec.rkt run-task resolves recipe->argv, injects env into a COPY (EXPORT_DIR steers output to explicit scratch dest; secret-injection hook point), runs via system*/exit-code with inherited stdio (basic streaming), flush before spawn for correct ordering. main.rkt --run <task>. Executed generate-sqlite for real: uv/3.14, exit 0, wrote 32MB occurrences.db to scratch. Verified beeatlas tree CLEAN and its real occurrences.db untouched (inputs read-only). Tests green (51).

title: Slice 2a — hermetic recipes + dry-run executor
description: Populate each task's invoke slot with a structured recipe (runtime-tag + args), transcribed from run.py imports; declare the two runtimes (uv/3.14, dbt uvx/3.13); print the exact ordered shell commands for a target. No execution. See docs/adr/0002.
notes: DONE. src/exec.rkt (recipe/runtime structs, recipe->argv, shell-quoted dry-run printer); recipes populated on all 32 tasks in src/beeatlas.rkt; two runtimes declared; main.rkt --commands mode. Dry run emits 21 ordered commands for occurrences.db (20 uv/3.14, 1 uvx/3.13). Tests still green (51). Decision recorded in docs/adr/0002.

title: Slice 1b — re-express reachable-set as a Datalog reachability rule; cross-check against 1a
description: Re-express the reachable-set core of 1a as a Datalog reachability rule over the same graph facts. Keep both implementations and assert they agree (consistency test). Realizes the H0 'Datalog as metadata language about the build' commitment. See docs/adr/0001-build-graph-model.md.
notes: DONE. src/plan-datalog.rkt re-expresses required-tasks as a 3-rule Datalog reachability closure (datalog pkg, bottom-up — DESIGN-aligned). graph->theory loads produces/consumes/goal facts (runtime symbols spliced via #,), queries needed-task. Cross-check in plan-test.rkt: datalog-required-tasks ≡ required-tasks for 6 targets incl. intermediate, siblings, and an external leaf (empty set). 51 checks green. The hand recursion + visited-set of 1a collapses to 3 declarative rules.

title: Slice 1a — model beeatlas graph as Racket data; compute + print occurrences.db minimal upstream (plain Racket)
description: Author the full 33-node bipartite graph as Racket-native data (precise edges for occurrences.db upstream, coarse-but-honest for siblings). Compute the minimal upstream of occurrences.db in topological order and print it. No execution, no hashing. Validate: order respects run.py's hand-order; plan drops the ~15 siblings. See docs/adr/0001-build-graph-model.md.
notes:
Model core working end-to-end (src/model.rkt, src/beeatlas.rkt, src/main.rkt). Plain-Racket bipartite graph + minimal-upstream (backward reachability) + Kahn topo-sort + pruning. Verified on a real occurrences.db microcosm: gate-as-token, multi-output dbt opacity, and per-target pruning all behave correctly across 3 targets (occurrences.db, occurrences.parquet, species.json). REMAINING: broaden beeatlas.rkt from subset to full 33 nodes; add a validation test (order respects run.py edges; expected ~15 siblings prune).
DONE. Full 32-node beeatlas graph authored in src/beeatlas.rkt (precise occurrences.db-upstream edges from dbt sources.yml + run.py comments; coarse-but-honest siblings, coarse edges marked). Validation in src/plan-test.rkt (45 checks incl. order-independent topological-validity): occurrences.db plan = 21 upstream tasks, prunes exactly the 11 post-dbt steps (exports/maps/feeds/collectors/notes/dedup/topology). Honest finding: because dbt-build is one opaque task consuming ~all ingestion, nearly every pre-dbt step is upstream; pruning=the slow post-dbt tail. Finer pruning needs dbt decomposition (deferred, H1+).

title: Determinism-testing harness (build twice, compare hashes)
description: Build the same snapshot twice and compare hashes. Day-one task, not someday: DuckDB parallelism, floating point, and spatial joins all threaten determinism.
notes: DONE. src/determinism.rkt verify-determinism builds the plan (or --from suffix) twice into separate EXPORT_DIRs, sha1-hashes the target, compares. main.rkt --verify. Proven by catching a REAL case: occurrences.db is nondeterministic across builds (see st-aji). Harness delivered; the nondeterminism it found is tracked separately.

title: Per-task hermetic runtime invocation (dual-interpreter)
description: Invoke each task in its own hermetic runtime. Concrete case: Python 3.14 for dlt loaders, 3.13 for dbt (dbt-core hard-crashes on 3.14). Secrets inject hermetically and must never reach logs.
notes:
Mechanism DELIVERED: per-task hermetic runtime invocation works; proven end-to-end on the uv/3.14 path (generate-sqlite). dbt uvx/3.13 runtime is declared + executor is runtime-agnostic (validated by construction), but not yet exercised by a real dbt run. Remaining under this umbrella: spot-run the dbt/3.13 path; multi-task plan execution (partial success = st-d44.4). Leaving open until the dbt path is actually exercised.
COMPLETE. dbt-build executed through Stelis in the uvx/3.13 runtime: 128 dbt nodes, exit 0 (3 warns, 0 errors). Dual-interpreter now proven end-to-end — generate-sqlite under uv/3.14 AND dbt-build under uvx/3.13 via the same runtime-agnostic run-task. beeatlas tracked tree stayed clean (duckdb + dbt target/ are gitignored derived state). Fixed main.rkt to only report occurrences.db for generate-sqlite.

title: Target selection + up-to-date skipping
description: Name a target; run the minimal upstream; skip what's already current. Today the only build is 'run everything'; the immediate need was only occurrences.db.
notes: Target-selection (plan computation) delivered by 1a (src/model.rkt, plain Racket) + 1b (src/plan-datalog.rkt, Datalog), cross-checked equivalent. The 'up-to-date skipping' half is tracked under st-d44.3 (input-addressed caching); executing the selected plan is st-d44.2 (hermetic runtime). Closing as the selection/planning goal is met.

title: Retire run.py's linear sequence for the occurrences.db build
description: Horizon 0 exit condition: naming occurrences.db builds the minimal upstream, skips already-current work, and retires run.py's hand-sequenced list for that build — reproducibly, verified by the determinism harness. Scope guard: transformations stay external (orchestrate dlt/dbt/exporters; do not reimplement in Racket).
notes:
Graph model decided via grilling — see docs/adr/0001-build-graph-model.md. Bipartite (task+artifact) nodes; gates=token-producing tasks; ingestion=opaque boundary artifacts (un-addressed, boundary-stamped); identity=authored name, version=separate/deferred; graph authored as Racket-native data in stelis. Slice 1 = plan only (1a plain Racket, 1b Datalog), full 33-node graph, precise edges for occurrences.db upstream.
EPIC COMPLETE (Horizon 0 build-system capabilities). Delivered: plan (bipartite graph, minimal-upstream, plain Racket + Datalog) -> dry-run commands -> execute per-task in hermetic runtimes (uv/3.14 + uvx/3.13) -> ordered plan execution with partial success + explicit output destinations -> input-addressed skip-if-current caching (derived/authoritative) -> streaming per-task logs -> determinism harness. Exit conditions: occurrences.db minimal-upstream rebuild PROVEN REPRODUCIBLE (byte-identical; beeatlas-zo7 fixed + verified); run.py's linear sequence retired for the derived tail. The only thing not run end-to-end is live ingestion (needs secrets — deferred, not a Stelis capability gap).

title: Edge-verification gate: check a task's declared inputs are sufficient and outputs complete
description: THROUGH-LINE from st-4cm slices 1-3: every post-dbt edge is a hand-transcribed (lossy) copy of what the Python task actually reads/writes, and it stays subtly wrong until a target exercises it. Each slice rediscovered the same class of bug (sandbox-vs-@export inputs, missing inputs, an undeclared output) the same way: run the task hermetically, watch a FileNotFoundError, or notice an extra written file. Mechanize that loop into a check. Task already runs in a controlled EXPORT_DIR, so: (a) INPUTS SUFFICIENT — seed the hermetic run with ONLY the task's declared inputs and assert it succeeds (a missing declared input = under-approximated edge); (b) OUTPUTS COMPLETE — assert it wrote exactly its declared outputs, no more/less (catches undeclared outputs like place_details.json in slice 3). Converts per-target archaeology into a red/green gate; makes under-declared inputs AND outputs impossible to miss. Retro-validate the shipped targets (occurrences.db, species.json, collectors.json, places.json). Highest-leverage of the three cleanups. Horizon check: batch, provenance-adjacent, no change-over-time = Horizon 1 in character; the user is the one paying the transcription tax, so not premature-for-lack-of-a-user, but confirm scope before building (edges toward a general edge-verification feature).
close_reason: Built edge-verify.rkt harness: runs a task in a fresh EXPORT_DIR seeded with ONLY its declared @export inputs, asserts it still succeeds (inputs sufficient) and wrote exactly its declared outputs (outputs complete). Fixed-path inputs (sandbox/DB/seeds) are ambient. Pure classify-outputs + export-dir-artifact? unit-tested (edge-verify-test.rkt); gated integration test retro-validates the 4 shipped terminals (edge-verify-integration-test.rkt, skips w/o a reference build). Retro-validation FOUND a real slice-1 gap: species-export writes 6 files, edge declared 2 — added seasonality/photos/species_hosts/higher_taxa.json outputs+artifacts+paths; now 4/4 clean. No CLI (per decision). 224 tests pass.

title: Incremental rebuild via early cutoff: stop propagation on identical output
description: Second H1 slice (ROADMAP.md). Today a task whose input CHANGED always reruns everything downstream, even if its rebuilt output is byte-identical to before. Outputs are already content-hashed (determinism harness); extend the cache entry with output hashes and, after a task runs, compare — if outputs are unchanged, downstream tasks see unchanged inputs and cache-skip naturally. Watch the derived-vs-authoritative rule: cutoff applies only to derived outputs. Sequenced after the provenance epic so --explain can show exactly where cutoff triggered ('reran, output identical, downstream skipped').
acceptance_criteria: A change that rebuilds an intermediate to identical content does not rerun downstream tasks; --explain reports the cutoff point; determinism harness still passes.
close_reason: Cutoff is emergent from input-addressing (run-plan re-decides each task after upstreams ran); implemented the receipt: cache entry v3 stores per-output content hashes (derived+resolvable only, per the derived-vs-authoritative rule), post-run comparison yields an output-delta ('identical = the cutoff point) recorded on trace v3, printed during --build and rendered by --explain --last. Snapshot-less tasks (boundary, dbt) now store outputs-only entries so their reruns have a comparison basis. End-to-end acceptance test in src/exec-test.rkt; ADR 0003.

title: Slice 4 — record the build trace: --explain answers about the last real build
description: Slices 1–3 explain a hypothetical (dry-run) build. This slice persists what a real --build actually decided and did — per-task decision, outcome (ok/cached/failed/skipped), and fingerprints — as a small versioned trace file under .stelis/ (derived, disposable state, same rules as the cache sidecars). '--explain --last' then answers 'why DID that rebuild?' from the trace. This is deliberately the thin end of H1 trace/graph persistence: choose the record shape so the fuller persistence work can adopt it rather than replace it.
acceptance_criteria: After a --build, --explain --last reports each task's actual decision and outcome from the persisted trace without re-fingerprinting; trace format is versioned and a stale/unparseable trace degrades gracefully.
close_reason: run-plan records per-task decision+outcome; trace.rkt persists them versioned under .stelis/; --explain --last replays without re-fingerprinting; graceful degradation tested; 151 tests pass

title: Slice 3 — provenance as Datalog: stale/why-stale as rules over decision facts
description: Third Datalog application. Emit slice 1's decision records as Datalog facts (input-changed(T,A), boundary(T), cached(T), depends(T,U), ...) and define staleness by rules — e.g. stale(T) :- input-changed(T,_); stale(T) :- depends(T,U), stale(U) — so 'why is X stale?' becomes a query whose derivation IS the provenance chain, transitively (slice 2 only explains one hop). Follow the slice-1a/1b pattern from st-d44: cross-check the Datalog answer against the plain-Racket explanation on the beeatlas graph and on synthetic graphs. Keep it metadata-only per the H0 commitment: Datalog reasons ABOUT the build, transformations stay external.
acceptance_criteria: A Datalog query answers stale?/why-stale? for any task with a full transitive chain; answers cross-checked against the plain-Racket path in tests.
close_reason: staleness as Datalog rules over decision facts; blames/2 gives the transitive chain; --why CLI renders it as a tree; cross-checked against walk-explanations on synthetic + beeatlas graphs; 141 tests pass

title: Streaming per-task observability
description: Streaming, per-task logs by default. Secrets must never reach logs.
notes: DONE. run-task #:label captures the child's stdout/stderr and re-emits each line prefixed with the task name (streaming, per-task, real-time; separate pump threads so neither stream deadlocks). run-plan labels every task; single --run stays unlabeled (inherits stdio). Demonstrated: '  generate-sqlite │ ...'. 56 tests.

title: Gates as first-class nodes; partial success; explicit output destinations
description: Gates/assertions as first-class, cacheable, skippable graph nodes. Partial success: a failed target must not fail unrelated already-satisfied targets. Explicit output destinations — no hidden copies.
notes: DONE. run-plan (exec.rkt) executes an ordered plan with partial success on a pure blockers-of core (failed producer blocks dependents; ok doesn't; unrelated failures don't). main.rkt --build <target> [--from <task>]. Explicit output destination via EXPORT_DIR (scratch). Demonstrated: --build --from dbt-build occurrences.db ran dbt-build[uvx/3.13] -> generate-sqlite[uv/3.14] in order, 2 ok, output to scratch, beeatlas tree clean. Partial-success semantics unit-tested (54 checks). Gates execute as ordinary tasks (a nonzero exit -> 'failed -> blocks dependents); no gate sits on the runnable derived tail so gate-failure isn't live-demoed, but the mechanism is uniform + tested. --from is a temporary suffix-bound until skip-if-current (st-d44.3) lands.

title: Input-addressed task caching + derived-vs-authoritative escape hatch
description: Cache task results by content hash of inputs. Derived outputs are safe to destroy and rebuild; authoritative state is forward-only (migrations only, never rebuilt from scratch). The caching model must express both node types.
notes: DONE (first increment). Input-addressed skip-if-current caching. src/cache.rkt: input-fingerprint = sha1(recipe + per-input-file content hashes); returns #f for tasks whose inputs aren't fully content-addressable (duckdb relations/tokens) — those always run. Disposable versioned sidecars in gitignored .stelis/cache/ (format churn -> cache miss, never breakage; safe to rm). run-plan (exec.rkt) skips a task as 'cached when fp matches AND outputs still exist; blockers-of treats 'cached as satisfied. Derived-vs-authoritative: added provenance field to artifacts (model.rkt), notes.json marked authoritative (addresses code-review #5); the escape-hatch runtime guard activates when an authoritative output is on a runnable path (none on occurrences.db). Demonstrated on generate-sqlite: cold->runs, warm->cached, touch(mtime)->still cached (content-addressed), output-deleted->re-runs. dbt-build stays uncached (duckdb inputs, per ADR-0001 Q4 / H2 snapshotting). 56 tests. Advances epic exit-condition 'skips current work'.

title: Fail loudly when a declared target output resolves to no path (beeatlas-path #f)
description: From st-4cm slice-3 review. beeatlas-path/case maintains a terminal-export enum [(occurrences.db species.json collectors.json places.json ...)] that is appended per slice. Forgetting to add a new target does NOT error — it falls to [else #f], silently making the artifact uncacheable and unverifiable; you only notice via the enum. A DECLARED TARGET OUTPUT whose path resolves to #f should be a hard error at build/plan time, not a silent skip. Small, pure-safety guardrail. Partly subsumed by the placement-as-property refactor but worth doing independently since it is trivial and removes a real footgun.
close_reason: Guard added: check-file-outputs-resolvable (cache.rkt) rejects any plan whose 'file output resolves to #f, wired into --build and --verify (main.rkt). feeds now errors loudly; shipped targets pass. plan-test #11 covers both cases.

title: Model artifact placement as a property, not a scattered convention
description: From st-4cm slice-3 review. The sandbox<->export-dir duality is currently expressed in FIVE parallel places in beeatlas.rkt: mart-files, export-name, the @export regex in beeatlas-path, the hand-maintained terminal-export enum in beeatlas-path/case, and the cp loop in place-marts-script. Key fact: the @export copy is byte-identical to its sandbox original BY CONSTRUCTION (place-marts is a plain cp) — so this is not a content distinction, it is pure PLACEMENT. Fold it: let a file artifact declare where it lives (sandbox / export-dir / raw) and, for a placed copy, which sandbox artifact it copies. Then place-marts, the @export artifacts, and their on-disk paths all DERIVE from that one declaration instead of being maintained in parallel. Fewer edits per new target; the sandbox-vs-export distinction becomes explicit instead of hiding in a string suffix. RECOMMENDATION: hold until the edge-verification gate exists — that gate will reveal whether the placement model captures the right things before this refactor commits to a shape.

title: --verify harness: derive out-file from target + handle post-dbt suffix inputs
description: verify-determinism (src/determinism.rkt) hardcodes out-file default 'occurrences.db' and main.rkt never passes the target as out-file, so --verify only meaningfully works for occurrences.db. Two gaps for post-dbt targets like species.json/collectors.json/places.json: (1) out-file should be derived from the target artifact's on-disk name (beeatlas-path); (2) --verify --from <suffix> builds into a fresh temp dir that lacks the upstream @export inputs (place-marts copies, species.parquet@export), so a suffix build fails with FileNotFoundError. Either build the full plan for post-dbt targets, or seed the clean dir with upstream outputs. Surfaced while building+verifying places.json (st-4cm slice 3); determinism there was instead evidenced by byte-identical direct-invocation parity + deterministic cache-skip.

title: place-marts node: place dbt marts in EXPORT_DIR; wire collectors.json (st-4cm slice 2)
description: st-4cm slice 2. Post-dbt exports read the dbt marts from EXPORT_DIR/*.parquet, but Stelis's dbt-build recipe (run.sh build only) omits run.py's _run_dbt_build copy of 6 marts (occurrences.parquet, occurrence_places.parquet, counties/ecoregions/wilderness.geojson, checklist.parquet) sandbox->EXPORT_DIR. Model that placement as an explicit 'place-marts' node (st-d44.4 explicit output destinations) producing @export artifacts, leaving the occurrences.db path (sandbox-direct) untouched. Then wire+verify collectors.json (reads EXPORT_DIR occurrences.parquet from place-marts + species.parquet from species-export). Fixes collectors-export's modeled inputs (pointed at sandbox occurrences.parquet; should be the @export copy + species.parquet@export).
acceptance_criteria: place-marts copies the 6 marts to EXPORT_DIR and cache-skips on rerun; --build collectors.json produces collectors.json byte-identical to a direct run; occurrences.db plan unchanged except place-marts joins the pruned set. Tests updated.
close_reason: place-marts node placing 6 dbt marts to EXPORT_DIR shipped; collectors.json built+verified byte-identical to a direct invocation; place-marts/species-export/collectors-export all cache-skip on rerun; fixed geojson resolution gap (surfaced by place-marts never caching) and collectors-export's sandbox-vs-@export input fidelity. occurrences.db plan unchanged (place-marts pruned). 199 tests pass.

title: Verify species.json as a second built+verified target (st-4cm slice 1)
description: First concrete slice of st-4cm (broaden target coverage, H1). Stelis already PLANS any terminal, but only occurrences.db has been built+verified end-to-end. Take species.json (species-export cone) as a second real target: make its output resolve to a real path, confirm the species_export recipe runs in its hermetic runtime and produces correct output, and exercise cutoff/provenance on an independent post-dbt path. Scope guard: this is the small-increment forcing function for graph-authoring ergonomics, not the full run.py sweep (that's a later slice / the aggregate-target option).
acceptance_criteria: racket src/main.rkt --build --from species-export species.json runs species-export in its runtime and produces species.json at a Stelis-resolved path; a second run cache-skips it; --explain/--why report it correctly. run.py parity spot-checked.
close_reason: species.json built+verified E2E: byte-identical (4b987c55) to production public/data/species.json AND to a direct uv invocation; second run cache-skips; --why/--explain --last report it; fixed missing occurrences.parquet input on species-export; regression test added. All 191 tests pass.

title: Per-column relation digests — attribute-level provenance substrate
description: st-d5d shipped the ROW-COHERENT relation digest (sum of per-row md5) — enough for correct cutoff on the pre-dbt graph. The recorded st-d5d design also called for PER-COLUMN digests + non-null counts riding along in the same query, as the substrate for modeling which artifacts derive from which attributes (DESIGN.md: 'attributes as functions of attributes'). That half was deferred: nothing consumes per-column facts yet, and storing them means bumping the cache receipt format. Pick up when an attribute-level consumer exists (e.g. --why narrowing to changed columns, or attribute-level incrementality). relation-digest.rkt's single-table query is shaped so the per-column terms slot in there; the cache decision still consumes ONE combined hash. Keep the row-coherent term as the correctness guarantee — per-column multiset digests alone false-skip on cross-row value swaps (proven in the st-d5d design discussion).
design: Row-coherent term stays the skip signal. Per-column terms are recorded in the receipt for downstream queries only. NULL-drop-out of sum/count is covered by emitting count(col) alongside each column digest.

title: Content-address db-relations so cutoff reaches the pre-dbt graph
description: Today every db-relation input yields 'inputs-unresolvable, so all pre-dbt tasks always rerun and early cutoff (st-8ig) only has teeth on file edges around dbt-build. Give relations a content hash so input-addressing — and therefore cutoff — covers the whole graph.
design: Hash in place via DuckDB CLI (-readonly), built-ins only — no extensions (community extensions add INSTALL/network/supply-chain surface; built-in hash() is version-unstable per duckdb#17596, md5_number is fixed MD5). Digest query is GENERATED PER RELATION from information_schema.columns (EXCLUDE hard-fails on absent columns; dlt bookkeeping varies 0/2/4 cols per relation — exclude column_name LIKE '_dlt%'). Hybrid shape, one query per relation: (a) row-coherent term: sum(md5_number_lower(to_json(struct_pack(cols…))::VARCHAR)) — catches cross-row value swaps that per-column multiset digests miss (false-skip = stale downstream, unacceptable); (b) per-column digests sum(md5_number_lower(col::VARCHAR)) + count(col) per column, plus count(*) — NULL-drop-out covered by counts; substrate for future attribute-level provenance (DESIGN.md: attributes as functions of attributes; incrementality itself stays deferred). sum not bit_xor (bit_xor cancels duplicate rows). Cache decision consumes ONE hash (hash of the canonical serialized result row); per-column detail rides along in the receipt. Run between tasks (db write-locked during loaders/dbt). Measured on widest relation (occurrences, 46k×107): ~2s, deterministic across runs; typical relations well under 0.5s. Resolver contract question stands: generalize name->path to name->hash, keeping DuckDB access at the boundary. Alternative (parquet snapshot exports) deferred — H2 salishsea ingestion-snapshot model.
acceptance_criteria: A rerun of a loader that lands identical logical content lets its downstream transform cache-skip; --explain names relation inputs as hashed, not unresolvable; determinism harness unaffected.
notes: SHIPPED (row-coherent digest). relation-digest.rkt: DuckDB CLI (-readonly), sum(md5_number_lower(to_json(row))) with dlt columns excluded via COLUMNS(lambda c: NOT starts_with(c,'_dlt_')); multi-table relations combined in sorted order; unreadable -> #f -> conservative rerun. Wired through build-env resolve-relation + input-snapshot kind-dispatch; beeatlas.rkt carries the verified logical->physical table mapping. Acceptance met: (1) hermetic test proves unchanged relation -> skip, changed -> rerun; (2) --explain against live db now shows anti-entropy/resolve-taxon-ids/resolve-checklist-names/inactive-remap as 'no cache entry' (hashed) not 'inputs not content-addressable'; dbt-build correctly names only its 4 gate-TOKEN inputs as unresolvable; (3) determinism tests pass (digest is read-only). Per-column digests deferred to st-7vz. NOTE: dbt-build itself stays uncacheable due to gate tokens — a separate concern (token content-addressing).
close_reason: Row-coherent db-relation content-addressing shipped and verified; per-column substrate split to st-7vz

title: Broaden target coverage beyond occurrences.db
description: H1 item (ROADMAP.md): run.py still exists in beeatlas and is only retired for the occurrences.db target. Author more of the beeatlas pipeline as graph nodes (candidate next targets: checklist pipeline, coverage basemaps, exports) so more of run.py's linear sequence retires. Good forcing function for graph-authoring ergonomics in beeatlas.rkt; pairs well with provenance for debugging new task authoring.
notes: Slice 3 DONE (st-4cm): places.json built+verified. places-export edge corrected — reads @export copies from EXPORT_DIR, not sandbox (same Pitfall-5 shape as slice 2 collectors): inputs now occurrence_places.parquet@export + occurrences.parquet@export + species.parquet@export + geographies_places (was only sandbox occurrence_places.parquet + geographies_places). Added place_details.json as the third output (one places_export invocation writes places.geojson + places.json + place_details.json). Added places.json/places.geojson/place_details.json to beeatlas-path terminal case (else unresolvable). All three outputs byte-identical to a direct 'uv run python places_export.py' on the same @export inputs (places.json sha b92fe946, 34202 bytes; geojson c0aa1b2f; details 6cdb7be2); second run cache-skips; --why/--explain --last report correctly. NOTE: differs from production public/data/places.json (33985 bytes, Jul 10) due to input drift — direct-invocation parity is the right check (as with collectors.json). plan-test gains a places.json guard (test #10) + places.json in the datalog-closure list. --verify unusable for post-dbt suffix targets → filed st-dtq. Slices 1+2 (species.json, place-marts/collectors.json) still UNCOMMITTED in working tree; slice 3 stacks on them. Remaining terminals: feeds/species-maps/place-maps (dir outputs), topology (in-place geojson rewrite), collectors-events-export (extends collectors.json in place), notes-harvest (authoritative). Two output shapes still need a decision: directory outputs and in-place-extend outputs.

title: Data-quality Datalog: rule-sets that run as nodes within the build
description: H1 item (ROADMAP.md): the second Datalog application domain — data-quality checks (duplicate sample IDs per collector-day, out-of-state samples, bee-vs-flower misclassification) expressed as rule-sets that execute as first-class nodes in the build graph, with gate semantics from st-d44.4. Deferred until a target is pulling on it; transformations otherwise stay external (H0 commitment).

title: Trace/graph persistence: build state moves from memory to a database/log
description: H1 item (ROADMAP.md): persist the build graph and run traces beyond the single last-build trace file introduced in st-yg7.4, using the representation H0 was designed to allow. Deliberately AFTER provenance: what provenance queries need is what's worth persisting. Scope and storage choice (log vs db) to be decided when picked up.
